role: First Minister of Defence of Islamic Republic; co-founder of Amal Movement in Lebanon
bio: Engineer, guerrilla fighter, and political figure. Helped organize the [Freedom Movement of Iran](/orgs/freedom-movement-of-iran) from the United States. In 1971 he left the US for Lebanon, where he became the close associate and military right-hand man of [Musa al-Sadr](/people/musa-al-sadr). Together they co-founded the [Amal Movement](/orgs/amal-movement) in 1975 as the military wing of the Movement of the Deprived. Chamran established training camps in southern Lebanon near the Israeli border, where he trained hundreds of Shia fighters — and, by his own account, Iranian volunteers as well. His training infrastructure, fighter networks, and organizational methods in Lebanon laid critical groundwork for the later emergence of [Hezbollah](/orgs/hezbollah) after the 1982 Israeli invasion. After the [Iranian Revolution](/events/iranian-revolution-1979), Chamran returned to Iran and served as the first post-revolution Minister of Defence. He fought on the front lines in the [Iran–Iraq War](/events/iran-iraq-war) and was killed in action on 21 June 1981 in Dehlavieh, Khuzestan.
editorial_notes: KIA 21 June 1981. First post-revolution Defence Minister.
